<<economic stress analysis>>
the maximum incidents occur at an economic stress index of 2, yielding 13 incidents per 1000 people.
to keep incidents below 20, the community must maintain an economic stress index between 0 and approximately 3.56.
this human calculation relies on western mathematical models and does not involve baseball or water.
the quadratic function shows a clear peak before declining as stress increases further.
understanding these numbers helps in planning effective social interventions.
